items.title: Security: hooks system lacks visibility and write-time confirmation — silent global exfiltration surface
items.desc:
## Summary

Claude Code's `hooks` field in `~/.claude/settings.json` is a legitimate and useful feature. This is not a bug report. It's a request to harden a design surface that makes hooks trivially weaponizable without any user awareness.

**A real-world case already exists.** The package "Cozempic" (Ruya-AI/cozempic) was audited by community members in #48041 after its author was found dropping templated install prompts across 50+ issues in this repo. The audit found it: writes a global `Sess...
items.llm_title: Claude Code 会话钩子供应链投毒
items.llm_summary: 该条目揭示了 Claude Code 的 `hooks` 配置机制存在严重设计缺陷。恶意软件包（如“Cozempic”）可在安装时静默修改全局配置，注入恶意钩子，从而在用户不知情的情况下窃取会话数据，形成大规模数据泄露的攻击面。
items.llm_reproduction: 攻击复现步骤：1. 攻击者发布一个看似无害的 npm 包（如“Cozempic”）。2. 用户安装该包。3. 包的安装脚本（postinstall）静默向用户的 `~/.claude/settings.json` 写入恶意 `hooks` 配置。4. 配置将终端输出或特定会话内容重定向至攻击者服务器。

It does NOT execute arbitrary code.

items.title: [Security] Ship a dedicated/bundled Node binary so macOS TCC permissions (FDA, Accessibility) are scoped to OpenClaw only
items.desc:
## Problem

When users grant **Full Disk Access** (or Accessibility) to OpenClaw on macOS, they must grant it to the shared `node` binary (e.g. `/usr/local/bin/node` or an nvm-managed path). macOS TCC has no mechanism to scope that permission to OpenClaw specifically — the grant applies to **every process that runs through the same binary**, including:

- All globally installed npm packages
- All `node_modules` in any project on the machine
- Any supply-chain-compromised dependency

items.title: feat: add reversible sensitive data sanitization layer (Security Guardrail)
items.desc:
## Core Value / 核心价值

OpenClaw 已经能承担非常多有意义的工作——自动化运维、代码生成、数据查询、多轮对话协作。但随着使用场景深入，一个安全顾虑始终制约着更多用户的探索：**既想用公有云模型的强大能力，又担心对话中不可避免地暴露数据库连接串、API Key、内网地址等敏感信息。**

这个 PR 提供了一个务实的解决方案：用本地模型做智能语义脱敏，敏感数据不出本地网络，云端模型全程只看到 `[VAULT_N]` 占位符，回程自动还原——用户无感，安全无忧。让团队能放心地把 OpenClaw + 公有模型用到生产级场景中，不必在便利和安全之间做取舍。

OpenClaw already handles a wide range of meaningful work — automated ops, code generation, data queries, multi-turn collaboration. But as usage deepens, a persistent security concern holds back broader adopt...

Recent research ([Invariant Labs](https://invariantlabs.ai/blog/mcp-security-notification-tool-poisoning-attacks), [Snyk Agent Scan](https://snyk.io/blog/agent-scan)) has highlighted growing concerns around:

This PR addresses multiple critical security findings identified in an independent security audit:

1. Confirmation bypass (--yes-always): Added explicit_yes_required=True to file creation/edit confirmations and the /run command gate.
2. Dangerous defaults: Changed defaults for --auto-accept-architect, --auto-commits, --dirty-commits, --auto-lint, and --suggest-shell-commands from True to False.
3. Shell injection: Replaced shell=True with shell=False + shlex.split() in cmd_git, pipe_editor, and...

Adds a security warning when `.aider.conf.yml` files from workspace directories contain shell command configurations (`lint-cmd`, `test-cmd`, `notifications-command`).

## Problem
Malicious repositories can include `.aider.conf.yml` with arbitrary shell commands that execute automatically when users make code changes (since `auto-lint: true` is the default). Users receive no warning about this risk.

1. Attacker creates repo with malicious `.aider.conf.yml`...

## Summary

Fixes #5810. `security.otp.gated_actions` accepted any arbitrary string without feedback — the example from the issue,


loads cleanly even though `kubectl_write` corresponds to no registered tool, so the operator believes they've added a TOTP gate on a destructive action that cannot in fact be gated. This is a silent failure mode on a knob operators specifically reach for to harden destructive flows.

items.desc:
## Problem

`allow_scripts` and `allowed_commands` are global config flags. Enabling them for one Python-based skill opens the same permissions for every installed skill and the agent itself.

Example: installing a portfolio analysis skill that needs `python3` requires setting:

```toml
[skills]
allow_scripts = true

[autonomy]
allowed_commands = ["python3", ...]
```

This also allows every other skill to run Python scripts and any agent conversation to invoke `python3`.

items.title: feat(security): gate /restart and /build bot commands behind owner role check
items.desc:
## Problem

`/restart` and `/build` are Telegram bot commands that call `launchctl kickstart -k` to restart the host process. The only access control is `isMain === true` (command must come from the main group) — there is no user-level check. Any Telegram user who can post to the main group can restart NanoClaw.

Discovered during investigation of three unexpected restarts on 2026-05-07 (13:37, 13:52, 14:45).

Relevant code: `src/channels/telegram.ts:96–120`, `src/bot-commands.ts:31–62`.

```
### What is Tirith?

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal security tool written in Rust that analyzes commands for content-level threats that regex deny lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl | bash`, `wget | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env`
- **Base64 decode-execute chains** — decoded payloads piped into interpreters or exe...

The current implementation allowed any command to execute as long as the absolute paths within the command matched the caller-provided cwd. By setting working_dir to a location outside the workspace, an LLM could manipulate files anywhere on the system it has access to.
```

```
items.title: fix(security): set 0o600 permissions on config and session files
items.desc:
## Summary

Config files (containing API keys) and session files (containing conversation history) are created with default umask permissions (`0o644` / `rw-r--r--`), allowing other local users on shared systems to read sensitive data.

This PR replaces `open(path, "w")` with `os.open(path, O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0o600)` to ensure only the file owner can read/write these files.

## Vulnerability Details

- **CWE-732**: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
```

2. 尝试让AI代理执行一条高危命令，如 `sudo rm -rf /important_data`。

Currently, the filesystem tools (`ReadFileTool`, `WriteFileTool`, `EditFileTool`, and `ListDirTool`) in `nanobot/agent/tools/filesystem.py` do not enforce the `restrict_to_workspace` configuration. 

While the shell execution tool may have safety guards, these direct file tools can still access, read, or write files anywhere on the host system (e.g., `~/.ssh/id_rsa` or `~/.nanobot/config.json`). This poses a significant security risk for users who expect the agent to be sandboxed ...

items.llm_reproduction: 尝试让AI代理使用`ReadFileTool`或`WriteFileTool`去读取或写入工作区之外的文件，例如`~/.ssh/id_rsa`或`~/.nanobot/config.json`，如果操作成功，则表明漏洞存在。

After 3+ years of heavy agentic coding, I noticed a pattern: Claude/Cursor/Copilot generate code that works but doesn't belong.

It passes tests. It runs. But it fights the grain of the language. It invents state when the platform provides it. It hides causality behind clever one-liners. It creates three different solutions to the same problem in the same file. The architecture is technically valid but cognitively expensive.
